name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
overview: A scheme that gives monthly money directly to senior citizens from poor families to help with their daily needs.
eligibility: You are 60 years old or older.
Your family is below the poverty line (BPL card holder).
You do not receive any other government pension.
benefits: People aged 60 to 79 get 200 rupees from the central government plus state contribution (typically 1,000 to 2,000 rupees total) every month.
People aged 80 and above get 500 rupees from the central government plus state contribution (typically 1,500 to 3,000 rupees total) every month.

documents.name: Aadhar Card
documents.purpose: To prove your age and who you are.

documents.name: BPL Card
documents.purpose: To prove your family needs financial help.

documents.name: Bank Passbook
documents.purpose: To send the monthly money directly to your account.

documents.name: Passport Photo
documents.purpose: To attach to your application form.

steps.explanation: Find your Aadhar card, BPL card, and bank passbook. Take clear photos of them with your mobile phone if applying online.

steps.action: Visit your nearest Gram Panchayat or Social Welfare Office
steps.explanation: Go to the local village office or block welfare office. Ask the officer for the 'Old Age Pension Application Form'. You can also ask a family member to download it online.
steps.voice_command: Say: 'Where is my nearest pension office?'

steps.explanation: Write down your name, age, address, and bank account number carefully. The welfare officer will help you fill it for free.
steps.voice_command: Say: 'Tell me how to fill the form'

steps.explanation: Hand over the filled form along with copies of your documents. Make sure to get a printed receipt or application number from the officer. This will help us track your pension status.

faqs.question: When will I get my money every month?
faqs.answer: The pension amount is sent directly to your bank account in the first week of every month. You do not need to go to the office to collect it.

faqs.question: What if my pension stops coming?
faqs.answer: If you don't receive it for two months, visit your bank to check if your Aadhar is properly linked to your account. You can also call our helpline at 1800-111-222.

faqs.question: Can both husband and wife apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, if both husband and wife are over 60 years old and your family holds a BPL card, both can apply and receive separate pensions.

common_mistakes.mistake: Providing a wrong bank account number or invalid IFSC code.
common_mistakes.solution: Double-check your bank passbook's front page. Take a clear photo of it and attach it to prevent typing errors.

common_mistakes.mistake: Forgetting to complete the yearly 'Life Certificate' validation.
common_mistakes.solution: Every year in November, visit your local bank branch or common service center to submit your biometric thumbprint to prove you are active.

contact.office: Social Welfare Department, Block Development Office (BDO)

name: Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana
overview: A safe government savings scheme by LIC where you save a one-time sum and get a guaranteed regular monthly income for 10 years.
eligibility: You are 60 years old or older.
You are a resident citizen of India.
benefits: Guaranteed monthly income of 7.4% per year for a full 10 years.
You get all your saved money back after 10 years.
If you pass away during the 10 years, all your saved money is safely returned to your spouse or children immediately.

documents.name: Aadhar Card
documents.purpose: To verify your age and details.

documents.name: PAN Card
documents.purpose: Required for tax and savings account setups.

documents.name: Bank Details
documents.purpose: A canceled check or passbook to deposit your monthly interest directly.

steps.explanation: You can invest a minimum of 1,50,000 rupees (which gives you 1,000 rupees every month) up to a maximum of 15,000,000 rupees.
steps.voice_command: Say: 'How much money should I invest?'

steps.action: Visit nearest LIC Office or website
steps.explanation: Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) runs this scheme. Visit the nearest LIC branch or ask a trusted person to open the LIC website.

steps.action: Fill out the PMVVY application form
steps.explanation: Provide your personal details, nominee details (who gets the money in your absence), and select 'Monthly' payout option.
Write a check for your selected investment amount and submit it with the form. Keep the official receipt very safe.
steps.voice_command: Say: 'How to pay the investment amount?'

faqs.question: Can I withdraw my money early in an emergency?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you or your spouse face critical illnesses, the government allows you to withdraw 98% of your money early.
faqs.voice_command: Say: 'Can I take my money out early?'

faqs.question: Do I have to pay taxes on the monthly income?
faqs.answer: The interest income is taxable based on your overall yearly income, but there is no service tax or GST on the scheme itself.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not naming a nominee in the form.
common_mistakes.solution: Always fill the nominee section with your spouse or child's name, so they receive the capital easily without legal delays.

name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
overview: A free health cover scheme that gives you up to 5 lakh rupees of free treatment every year in top government and private hospitals.
eligibility: Your family is listed in the SECC-2011 social census database.
Active members of BPL, dynamic auto-selection of disadvantaged households.
No limit on family size or age (highly beneficial for senior citizens).
benefits: Free medical treatment, operations, medicines, and hospital stay up to 5,00,000 rupees every single year.
Covers pre-existing diseases from day one (even if you have diabetes or heart disease already, it is fully covered).

documents.name: Aadhar Card
documents.purpose: To check your name in the beneficiary list.

steps.action: Check if your name is on the list
steps.explanation: Open the official portal pmjay.gov.in or click 'Am I Eligible' inside our helper. Input your mobile number and Aadhar card details.
Go to the nearest digital cyber kiosk or CSC in your village/town, or visit the Ayushman Helpdesk at any large government hospital.
Show your Aadhar card and place your thumb on the scanning machine to confirm your identity. The center operator will generate your card.
The operator will print a plastic laminated green-colored 'Ayushman Card' for you. Show this card at any listed hospital to get fully free treatment.

faqs.question: Do I have to pay anything to get this card?
faqs.answer: No, getting the card and the treatment is 100% free. If any hospital or agent asks for money, call the helpline 14555 immediately.

faqs.question: Which hospitals accept my Golden Card?
faqs.answer: All government hospitals and thousands of registered private hospitals accept it. Look for the 'Ayushman Mitra' desk at the entrance of any hospital.

common_mistakes.mistake: Going to a hospital that is not in the official Ayushman network.
common_mistakes.solution: Always ask the reception desk: 'Do you accept Ayushman PM-JAY card?' before getting admitted, unless it is an emergency.

contact.office: Ayushman Help Desk in any Empanelled Hospital
